name: Tung-po Golden Pork Feet
recipeInstructions:
------------------------
SEASONINGS:
5 Ginger Slice
2 tsp Salt
Peppercorns
1 tsp Sugar Color
1 tsp Pepper, ground
1/2 tbsp Wine
1 tbsp Soy Sauce
1. Scrape hair off the pork foot and rinse.
2. Chop into sections and remove bones.
3. Cook in boiling water briefly.
4. Add soy sauce to the pork foot.
5. Heat oil for frying.
6. Fry the pork foot until golden brown, then remove and drain.
7. Save some oil in the wok.
8. Add seasonings (ginger, salt, peppercorns, sugar color, pepper, wine, soy sauce).
9. Stir quickly and add stock and pork foot.
10. Simmer over a low flame for 1 hour.
11. Remove from heat; steam for 10 minutes.
12. Save the juice oozing out from the pork foot.
13. Place the pork foot upside down on the plate.
14. Stir-fry dried pickled cabbage with sesame oil.
15. Add the juice, cornstarch paste, and spring onion; mix well.
16. Pour over the pork foot.
17. Fry carrot and turnip balls for 3 minutes.
18. Garnish with the balls and add spinach.
Serve.
Note: Sugar color is made by frying granulated sugar and rock sugar with oil over a very small flame. It looks like soy sauce and helps deepen the color of the dish. Tung-Po is also known as Su Shih and is one of the Eight Great Scholars. From The Chinese Regional Cuisine Series, Szechwan Cooking. Posted by James Lor.
